description: SigningBackend::sign() (lib.rs:312-315) converts the entire message to a MaxBuffer and calls ctx.hash() once. MaxBuffer is limited to TPM2_MAX_DIGEST_BUFFER bytes (typically 1024). Messages larger than this limit fail with 'message too large for TPM hash'. The error is surfaced, but the trait contract (secretx-core::SigningBackend::sign) does not document any message size limit — callers of other backends (PKCS#11, AWS KMS) can sign arbitrarily large messages because those backends hash internally without size constraints. Either: (a) implement chunked hashing via TPM2_SequenceUpdate/SequenceComplete, or (b) hash the message in software (SHA-256) and pass the digest directly to ctx.sign() with a NULL-hierarchy ticket, or (c) document the limitation prominently. Option (b) is simplest and matches what most TPM2 tools do.
title: sign() limited to ~1024-byte messages with no chunked hashing
